item.title: The leadership change in US stocks
item.description: A market seeking direction

item.title: DeepSeek plans hiring spree in escalation of China’s AI talent war
item.description: Advertised roles suggest company focused on commercialising frontier research

item.title: Global markets fall as investors fret about AI demand
item.description: Apple’s price rises and fears over delay to OpenAI’s IPO send Asian bourses sharply lower

item.title: Rheinmetall gambled on Germany’s doomed warship project — and lost
item.description: Arms company’s €15bn frigate blow tests faith among investors

item.title: Binance to stop providing services to EU clients after failing to obtain licence
item.description: World’s biggest crypto exchange tells customers how to withdraw their money as MiCA rules set to come into force

item.title: Trump son set for Kalshi windfall after prediction platform gave him stake
item.description: Privately owned company’s valuation has soared as US administration has adopted a light-touch approach to the sector

item.title: How JPMorgan became Jamie Dimon
item.description: The 70-year-old is one of the longest-serving bosses in finance

item.title: How the DeepMind mafia brought the AI boom to London
item.description: The tech sector is buzzing in Britain. But can it ever be more than a US outpost?

item.title: Swatch seeks $170mn from Samsung over digital clones of its watches
item.description: Swiss watchmaker accuses technology group of ‘large-scale appropriation’ of luxury designs in London lawsuit

item.title: Elon Musk’s SpaceX plans Starlink mobile push into US consumer market
item.description: Move would test whether group can turn sky-high ambition into a mass-market phone business

item.title: Revolut scraps ‘remote-first’ working for graduate hires
item.description: Europe’s most valuable fintech will require junior staff to spend at least three days a week in the office from next year

item.title: Zambia’s state-owned miner seeks to revive London listing
item.description: ZCCM chief executive hopes to publish 2025 results and resume UK trading “maybe within two months”

item.title: Chinese carmakers ‘knocking at US gate’ from Mexico and Canada
item.description: Beijing-subsidised vehicles emerge as flashpoint in North American trade relationship

item.title: Companies rush to fire high earners before UK ends cap on unfair dismissal payouts
item.description: Labour government is removing £123,543 limit on compensation as part of package to boost workers’ rights

item.title: The AI factory: the rewiring of India's tech industry
item.description: Outsourced data services may not be enough for India to thrive in the AI era

item.title: How to win at AI (if you’re not the US or China), with AI minister Kanishka Narayan
item.description: Specialisation and research can give the UK leverage

item.title: Noel Tata: How we made fashion one of our biggest businesses
item.description: Noel Tata and P Venkatesalu on the fast-growing retail chain you have likely never heard of

item.title: Attack in Strait of Hormuz halts evacuation plan for stranded ships
item.description: International Maritime Organization announces pause after container ship was hit by ‘unknown projectile’

item.title: Trump administration asks OpenAI to stagger release of new model to vet users
item.description: The US Treasury, commerce department and other government offices request limited distribution of GPT 5.6

item.title: Dimon upends succession race at JPMorgan again
item.description: Longtime lieutenant Marianne Lake recently informed she was no longer a CEO contender as new top deputies are named

item.title: FT Legal Outlook
item.description: We look at how the industry is shaping its future in response to upheaval on many fronts — from AI to new types of law firm. Plus: the inaugural FT Law 50 listing of influential individuals

item.title: How AI is powering new law firm structures
item.description: Interest in a model that separates legal casework from other operations exploded alongside the new tech

item.title: Legal sector can use psychology to beat fear of AI
item.description: Understanding the feelings that get in the way of embracing tech is important

item.title: Trump’s tests of the US justice system
item.description: For the time being, it is just about holding

item.title: Wall Street law firms ponder fresh era of insider trading risks
item.description: Alleged illegal misuse of private deals information at elite law firms has shaken many

item.title: In-house lawyers aim to shed the grind, not the people
item.description: Legal teams find that AI opens up opportunities on top of shouldering repetitive drudge work

item.title: Law firms look for clear gains from AI
item.description: Despite increased spending on legal tech, barriers to its full embrace remain

item.title: Meet the FT Law 50: shaping the future
item.description: The individuals featured here have been selected for their influence on a legal sector in transition

item.title: Ethical AI rows open way to wave of litigation
item.description: Lawyers are at the forefront of debate and dispute over the technology’s lawful and responsible use

item.title: FirstFT: Sanae Takaichi sets out Japan’s $2.3tn investment roadmap
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: attack in Strait of Hormuz halts evacuation plan and Jamie Dimon promotes two potential successors
